text.title: BA Requirements Management Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in real applications of this methodology. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Requirements are edited in-place; no version history exists.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treats requirements as living documents without baselining.
antipattern.fix: Enforce semantic versioning on every requirement; bump version on any edit; keep diff history.

antipattern.symptom: Every CR is approved by default; the threshold is decorative.
antipattern.root-cause: Approver does not read the impact assessment.
antipattern.fix: Force a written rationale field on every decision; reject empty-rationale approvals.

antipattern.symptom: CR references a req_id that does not exist in the register.
antipattern.root-cause: CR is created from a downstream artefact (test case, design doc) without linking back.
antipattern.fix: Validate every CR.req_id against the register; reject orphans.
